body: This PR adds JULIA_CUDA_USE_COMPAT=false to both test and docs to get around the compatibility issues between CUDA drivers and older GPUs like Titan V GPUs

labels.description: runs preconfigured benchamarks and spits out timing
labels.name: benchmark performance

title: Set `JULIA_CUDA_USE_COMPAT=false` in tests

body:
`schedule.actuations` are saved in the checkpointer, however, when restoring from the checkpointer on main, if the checkpointing `TimeInterval` has been changed, the `actuations` actually refer to the previous time interval so they might be out of sync.

Specifically, if we have decreased the checkpointing time interval, the checkpointer will fire at each time step until the `actuations` have synched up with `clock.time`. Much worse, If we have increased the checkpointing time interval, the actuations become too large for the new interval, pushing the new actuation well far in the future (the checkpointer will not fire until clock.time has catched up with the actuations).

This PR fixes this bug by not restoring schedule's details if we happen to have a situation where the `interval` changed, leaving the schedule without actuations (actuations = 0) and then skips ahead to catch up with clock.time in the first actuation.
title: Bugfix `actuations` for `TimeInterval`

body:
In main we continue the tripolar grid's metric south of the limit using a `LatitudeLongitudeGrid`.
This is not necessarily right as the `LatitudeLongitudeGrid` is topologically different from the `TripolarGrid`. 
It is probably better to just keep the same metrics and copy the j-row over to the halos given that the last j-row is always bounded and we cannot reach -90 degrees anyways by construction

This PR also fixes a bug for which the metrics at `j == 1`, so _inside_ the prognostic domain, were substituted with the different halo metrics
title: Change behavior for halo metrics for a `TripolarGrid` + bugfix at `j == 1`

body:
Creates problems with CI. https://github.com/JuliaGeo/ConservativeRegridding.jl already has an Oceananigans extension, so what we were achieving with XESMF can be done with
```julia
using Oceananigans
using ConservativeRegridding
```

labels.description: Concerning a change which breaks the API
labels.name: breaking change :broken_heart:

title: Remove xesmf extension
